factors.name: Price Action - Momentum Loss
factors.summary: NAS100 down -2.73% in 24h and -1.47% in 4h, signaling sustained selling pressure after record highs earlier in the week. Move is in progress but not yet deeply extended.

factors.name: Fed Hawkishness & Real Yields
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr warned of risks from deregulation proposals, criticizing moves to 'considerably weaken bank regulation.' ECB stepping up as 'lead hawk' with rate hike primed—spillover hawkishness pressures long-duration growth assets.

factors.name: June Swoon / Seasonal Weakness
factors.summary: MarketWatch article explicitly warns 'Don't rule out a June swoon — the S&P 500 is pushing its limits.' Market stretched after strong earnings but sentiment turning cautious as rally runs into seasonal headwinds.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk - Iran War Escalation
factors.summary: War in Iran approaches 100-day mark with ongoing missile/drone exchanges. Container shipping rates up 109% since war started; stagflation narrative emerging via IATA commentary on rising jet fuel costs and rate hike risk.

factors.name: Tech Valuation Stretch & Derating Risk
factors.summary: Macro analysts and credit managers (DoubleLine, Oaktree) bracing for 'potential AI pain' and are buying defensive debt. Robo-advisors cannot profit from AI stock picks; earnings beat sentiment positive but valuations already priced in.

factors.name: Crypto Contagion & Risk-Off Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin worst weekly rout since FTX, down >50% from Trump-reelection highs; cryptos shed $390B. NQ-BTC correlation historically shows tech weakness when crypto crashes—heightened risk-off spillover into NDX.

factors.name: South Korea Overheating & Global Equity Caution
factors.summary: Investors hedging SK stock positions as market reaches 'hottest' valuations; Samsung/SK Hynix rate-hike sensitivity could trigger 15% correction. Signals broader EM/developed market caution and semiconductor appetite cooling.

factors.name: Earnings Season Strength (Residual Bullish Offset)
factors.summary: Strongest earnings season in years with Wall Street analysts rarely this bullish on individual names. However, this tailwind is being overwhelmed by macro headwinds and sentiment shifts.

factors.name: Upcoming Major Catalysts - Oracle Earnings & AI Narrative Risk
factors.summary: Oracle earnings next week could stabilize sentiment or accelerate selloff if guidance disappoints. AI capex narrative pivotal but no new bullish catalyst visible in news window.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain - Mixed Signals
factors.summary: Boeing securing 200-jet China order suggests capex resilience, but TSMC/Samsung export control tensions and SK overheating suggest incremental demand caution. No major supply shock but momentum broken.

screening.narrative: NAS100 has rolled over sharply after testing record highs earlier in the week, now down -2.73% in 24h as a confluence of macro headwinds (ECB hawkishness, Iran war stagflation, real yield re-pricing) and equity valuation caution overwhelm late-cycle earnings strength. Crypto collapse (-$390B in a week) and global risk-off sentiment in South Korea suggest growing unease about stretched growth multiples at a moment when Fed tightening is re-accelerating overseas. The move is in Phase 2 (underway but not yet exhausted), with June seasonal weakness narrative ('June swoon') gaining traction alongside earnings euphoria wearing off.
catalysts_ahead: Oracle earnings (early next week) — major AI capex narrative pivot point
ECB rate decision (imminent per news) — hawkishness spillover to USD/Treasury yields
CPI/PCE release (likely next 48-72h) — inflation surprise could re-price Fed terminal rate
FOMC meeting (mid-June window likely) — forward guidance on rate cuts/hikes critical
NVDA earnings / hyperscaler cloud capex commentary — semiconductor/AI demand barometer
China tech regulation or EV tariff escalation — NDX international revenue headwind
screening.risk_warning: Move is already in progress (-2.73% 24h); if Oracle/MSFT next-week earnings crush expectations and reaffirm cloud capex, sentiment could snap back and invalidate the SELL bias. Oversold technical setup (VIX now catching up per news) could trigger relief rally. Additionally, if Fed signals dovish pause or inflation data cools unexpectedly, real yields collapse and growth multiples re-expand, negating the hawkish macro narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 — avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of 29,200-29,300 resistance or a failed pop above 29,000 mid-week before re-shorting. For longs: wait for capitulation signal or oversold bounce setup (RSI <30) before covering shorts and buying dips toward 28,600 support.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: 4h down -1.47%, 24h down -2.73% — multi-hour sell-off already underway. Move is extended into Phase 3; late-stage chasing risky.

factors.name: ECB rate hike primed (this week)
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports ECB stepping up as 'lead hawk' with rate hike coming imminently. Rising real yields globally compress long-duration Nasdaq multiples.

factors.name: Real yields pressure
factors.summary: ECB tightening cycle + global stagflation signals (Iran war 100 days in, oil cost pass-through) push real yields higher, structurally bearish for mega-cap growth/AI stocks.

factors.name: Crypto correlation breakdown
factors.summary: Bitcoin cratering (worst weekly rout since FTX, $390B crypto shed this week per CoinDesk). BTC-NQ linkage suggests risk-off sentiment; equity volatility likely to persist.

factors.name: Earnings/forward guidance gap
factors.summary: Strong earnings season closing, but Wall Street macro soothsayers and equity analysts diverge sharply on forward outlook. Hyperscaler capex commentary next week critical (Oracle earnings highlighted).

factors.name: June swoon warning
factors.summary: MarketWatch explicitly warns of 'June swoon' risk; S&P 500 'pushing its limits.' Record highs followed by avalanche selling on Friday (this week). Overbought unwind in progress.

factors.name: South Korea hedge surge
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Korean bull market 'overheating' — investors hedging, paring crowded trades; rate hike threatens 15% correction. Tech supply chain (Samsung, SK Hynix) are NQ components.

factors.name: Fed deregulation risk
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr warns looser Wall Street bank rules 'considerably weaken' regulation in a financial boom. Macro policy uncertainty and potential systemic stress weigh on sentiment.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings near-term uncertainty
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in the immediate 24-72h window. Oracle next week will be key. Lack of near-term catalysts limits upside catalyst conviction.

factors.name: Dollar direction mixed
factors.summary: No major DXY move cited in news. ECB hiking may briefly support EUR, but USD strength from escalating geopolitical risk (Iran war, Israel-Lebanon) could offset. Neutral on NQ FX hedge.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is in the midst of a Phase 3 extended sell-off (-2.73% in 24h) following a record-high rally earlier in the week. The primary driver is a confluence of hawkish macro headwinds: ECB rate hike imminent this week, global real yields rising, and crypto contagion (BTC down sharply, risk-off tone). South Korean equity overheating and hedge flows suggest crowded longs are unwinding. While earnings season has been strong, forward guidance gaps and macro policy uncertainty (Fed deregulation debate, Iran war stagflation) are compressing growth multiples. June swoon narrative is now live.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (this week — ~June 12) → hawkish surprise = further NQ compression
Oracle earnings (next week) + cloud/AI capex guidance → critical for hyperscaler demand narrative
US CPI/Core CPI data (mid-June) → inflation surprise would re-price Fed terminal rate and compress multiples
FOMC minutes (if released) → any hawkish hints = additional selling
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings (dates TBD, likely late June) → mega-cap capex and margin commentary
China-Taiwan or geopolitical escalation → semiconductor supply chain shocks
Bitcoin/crypto stabilization (or further dump) → risk sentiment barometer for equities
screening.risk_warning: Move is extended; if NQ rebounds decisively through 29,200 on Monday with positive hyperscaler news or weaker-than-expected inflation print, SELL bias invalidates. Conversely, if ECB hike is dovish-er than priced or geopolitical tensions ease abruptly, support could be tested. Watch for capitulation volume on further drops — that could trigger mean reversion rally into 29,100–29,300.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already extended 2.73% down and into Phase 3 — wait for retest of 28,800–29,000 support zone before considering fresh short. If support breaks (28,600), next leg down likely 28,200–28,000. For longs, wait for confirmed reversal pattern + close above 29,300 with volume before re-engaging.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: NAS100 down 2.73% over 24h and 1.47% in last 4h, indicating sustained selling pressure. Move is extended into day 2, consistent with Phase 3 weakness.

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain Stress
factors.summary: Micron fell 13% on Friday; chip stocks rally 'finally reverses' per Bloomberg; Samsung and SK Hynix face rate-hike headwinds as South Korea stock market shows signs of overheating. Critical for NDX given NVDA, AVGO, MRVL weighting.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: ECB primed for rate hike this week (Bloomberg: 'ECB Steps Up as G7's Lead Hawk'), pushing real yields higher globally. Long-duration Nasdaq 100 components (TSLA, NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL) highly sensitive to real rate compression; rising rates = headwind for growth multiples.

factors.name: Geopolitical/Macro Uncertainty
factors.summary: Iran war approaching 100-day mark with 'no resolution in offing'; U.S. intercepts Iranian drones near Strait of Hormuz. Energy prices, risk premium, and capital allocation pressure equity risk appetite.

factors.name: Crypto Capitulation & Risk-Off Linkage
factors.summary: Bitcoin dumped all gains since Trump reelection, now down >50% from peak; crypto shed $390B in worst weekly rout since FTX collapse. High correlation between NQ and BTC volatility suggests risk-off sentiment bleeding into equities.

factors.name: Tech-Specific Headwinds: AI Bubble Concerns
factors.summary: DoubleLine and Oaktree buying credit to hedge AI boom turning to 'credit bust'; chatter on 'June swoon' and S&P 500 'pushing its limits'. Sentiment shifting from euphoria to caution among large asset managers.

factors.name: OpenAI IPO/Pivot (Bullish Counter-Narrative)
factors.summary: OpenAI readies 'Superapp' platform overhaul ahead of planned IPO later this year, signaling major AI platform consolidation. Positive for mega-cap AI narrative but unlikely to offset macro headwinds in near term.

factors.name: Fed Deregulation Criticism
factors.summary: Fed Governor Michael Barr warns of risks tied to looser Wall Street bank rules, criticizing recent regulatory rollbacks. Adds regulatory uncertainty backdrop, though not an immediate market shock.

factors.name: Earnings Catalyst Pipeline (Weak Near-Term)
factors.summary: Oracle earnings next week mentioned as potential rally driver, but no mega-cap earnings imminent this week. Tesla upgraded by analysts, but no fresh earnings or guidance from AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA to trigger reversal.

factors.name: Dollar & International Headwind
factors.summary: South Korea currency won at 2009 lows amid local market overheating; no fresh dollar action cited this window. NDX has heavy international revenue exposure; weak EM sentiment is a mild headwind but not dominant.

key_levels.support: 28,600–28,700 (recent intraday lows, likely test on further weakness)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 has entered a Phase 3 extended selloff after Friday's sharp reversal from record highs. The dominant drivers are: (1) rising real yields from ECB hawkishness, (2) semiconductor supply chain stress with Micron/SK Hynix weakness, (3) crypto capitulation signaling risk-off mood, and (4) macro concerns around Iran war and AI capex bubble turning to bust. While OpenAI's IPO pivot is a positive longer-term narrative, it cannot offset the confluence of near-term headwinds. The move is already 2.73% into the red; chasing further shorts into an extended decline risks whipsaw on any technical bounce or positive earnings surprise.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (this week, likely +25bps hike) — will ramp real yield expectations
Oracle earnings next week — tech bellwether on cloud capex demand
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings (dates TBD but likely next 2-4 weeks) — critical for Mag 7 validation
US CPI/PCE data (early June window typical) — inflation surprise will re-price Fed terminal rate expectations
FOMC meeting (TBD) — forward guidance on rate path post-Iran war and AI bubble concerns
South Korean policy response to won weakness & stock overheating — affects semiconductor supply chain confidence
World Cup opening (June 11) — macro risk-off sentiment could persist into weekly close
screening.risk_warning: If mega-cap earnings beat expectations next week (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL) with resilient forward guidance, or if ECB backtracks on rate-hike hawkishness, bias flips to BULLISH and shorts are liquidated. Also, if crypto stabilizes (BTC finds support >$60K) and risk sentiment reprices, NQ bounce could be sharp. Further macro deterioration (Iran escalation, China-Taiwan friction) or surprise Fed hawkishness would validate downside, but move is already extended—next entry point ideally 28,400–28,600 support or on a technical bounce to 29,100 resistance.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended and late to chase. Wait for pullback to 28,700–28,800 support or a failed retest of 29,100–29,200 resistance before re-engaging. Do not short into intraday weakness; look for a consolidation or intraday bounce first to identify lower-risk entry.

factors.name: Momentum & price action
factors.summary: NAS100 down -2.73% over 24h and -1.47% in 4h, indicating directional selling momentum. Move is in progress but not yet extended into late-phase territory.

factors.name: ECB rate hike cycle starting
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports ECB stepping up as G7's lead hawk with interest-rate hike primed for the coming week. Rising real yields in eurozone will compress tech multiples and strengthen EUR/USD, headwind for US growth equities.

factors.name: AI boom showing stress cracks
factors.summary: DoubleLine and Oaktree are defensive positioning, buying credit that performs if AI bubble deflates. Fed's Barr warns of deregulation risks in a financial boom. Sentiment on mega-cap AI capex sustainability is shifting cautious.

factors.name: Crypto correlation & risk-off sentiment
factors.summary: Bitcoin eyeing worst weekly rout since FTX collapse, shedding $390B in crypto market cap. BTC down >50% from Trump-era peak. Tech-heavy NAS100 typically correlates with crypto risk appetite on larger drawdowns.

factors.name: Geopolitical uncertainty unresolved
factors.summary: Iran war at 100+ days with little progress on peace deal; fresh sanctions threat from France; OPEC+ meetings and Middle East conflict creating energy volatility. Unresolved geopolitical risk keeps risk appetite subdued.

factors.name: Upcoming ECB decision Thursday
factors.summary: Major rate decision in <48h creates near-term chop; markets often consolidate into FOMC-equivalent events. Reduces tactical conviction even if directional bias leans bearish.

factors.name: Semiconductor & supply chain pressures
factors.summary: South Korea's stock market rally showing signs of overheating and margin calls; Samsung/SK Hynix exposure is huge for NAS100. South Korea considering rate hike, which could trigger 15% correction in their market per MarketWatch.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings calendar gap
factors.summary: No major NDX component earnings in the immediate 24-48h window. Tesla upgraded by analysts on AI moves, but lacks fresh catalysts this week to reignite growth premium.

factors.name: BIAS HISTORY continuity
factors.summary: Prior three reads (06/07 03:43, 07:54, 10:29) were all SELL or NEUTRAL with no confirmation triggered. Current -2.73% 24h move validates the bearish lean but also suggests move is in Phase 2.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is in a confirmed sell-off phase after a week of volatility and record highs earlier in the session. The confluence of ECB tightening, AI capex skepticism creeping into credit markets, and unresolved Iran geopolitics has tilted risk appetite bearish. Crypto contagion (BTC down 50% YTD, worst weekly rout since FTX) is reinforcing tech/growth equity weakness. South Korea's potential rate hike adds emerging-market stress, threatening the semiconductor/memory chip supply chain that underpins NAS100's earnings power.
catalysts_ahead: ECB interest-rate decision Thursday 2026-06-11 (major, hawkish repricing risk)
FOMC expectations re-anchor in wake of ECB move
CPI/PCE inflation data next week (June 11-13 window likely)
Mega-cap tech earnings guidance (week of June 14-20)
Iran war ceasefire talks outcome (ongoing, binary risk)
South Korea policy decision on rates and Samsung stabilization
screening.risk_warning: A hawkish ECB coupled with dovish Fed pivot OR a surprise peace deal in Iran could flip bias to NEUTRAL/BUY within 48-72h. Also, if mega-cap earnings beat and raise guidance (esp. MSFT, GOOGL on cloud capex), the selloff could reverse sharply; NAS100 is still near all-time highs on a nominal basis and leverage-driven short positions could squeeze.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2, avoid chasing shorts. Wait for retest of 29200-29500 resistance or fresh breakdown below 28600 with confirmed follow-through selling before adding bearish exposure; better entry on pullback or post-ECB stabilization if direction remains down.

factors.name: Friday selloff momentum
factors.summary: Nasdaq Composite posted largest one-day point drop on record Friday; Nasdaq down 2.73% in 24h. Move is in progress with -1.47% in 4h, indicating continuation risk into early week.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings/capex cycle
factors.summary: AAPL WWDC conference is Monday event (potential catalyst), but no earnings surprises in immediate window. OpenAI IPO positioning and SpaceX IPO hype could attract flows but also signal frothy valuations.

factors.name: Hedge fund crowding / deleveraging risk
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports Friday's sudden rout renews concerns that unwinding of crowded trades could exacerbate losses. Market structure suggests fragility after months-long rally into record highs.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk: Iran war escalation
factors.summary: Iran-US tensions persist 100 days into conflict; peace deal appears far off per Bloomberg. Airlines, energy costs creating margin pressure (IATA, Air France-KLM commentary); global airlines slash 2026 profit forecasts on fuel shock.

factors.name: Real yields / duration sensitivity
factors.summary: No fresh FOMC, CPI, or Treasury yield moves reported in last 24h. ECB rate hike primed for Thursday, but US real rate trajectory unclear—tech duration risk remains elevated but not newly triggered.

factors.name: Inflation/cost pressures
factors.summary: Resin shortage driving electronics price inflation; fuel costs cited as margin headwind across airlines and transport. Sticky inflation narrative emerging despite overall CPI cooling narrative.

factors.name: Mega-cap share issuance
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports AI-focused mega-caps raising fresh equity for capex; warns of potential 'more shares than buyers' dynamic. Could dilute returns and pressure multiples if capex ROI disappoints.

factors.name: VIX/volatility regime shift
factors.summary: Nasdaq vol index (VXN) likely spiked Friday on record point drop; crypto markets shed $390B in worst weekly rout since FTX. Risk-off sentiment has abruptly reversed months of 'animal spirits' rally.

factors.name: Prior analyst bias reads
factors.summary: SELL5/10, NEUTRAL5/10, SELL6/10 from past 6 hours—mixed but tilted cautious. No confirmation triggered suggests volatility rather than conviction move; watch for follow-through.

screening.narrative: The Nasdaq's record two-month rally hit a wall on Friday with the largest one-day point drop on record, erasing $1.8T in market cap across equities. Catalyst appears to be a combination of hedge-fund deleveraging, escalating Iran-war inflation concerns (fuel, supply chain), and renewed crowding risks in mega-cap longs. Early week trading (down 2.73% in 24h, -1.47% in 4h) suggests the move is in PHASE 2—momentum continuing but not yet fully extended. Mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) remains vulnerable to real-yield repricing and margin compression from sticky inflation.
catalysts_ahead: AAPL WWDC 2026 (Monday) — product announcements, AI integration roadmap
US CPI / Core CPI (mid-week) — will reset inflation expectations and Fed rate trajectory
ECB rate hike decision (Thursday) — potential hawkish shift, risk appetite headwind
US ADP, NFP (week of) — employment data critical for Fed pivot narrative
SpaceX IPO roadshow — mega-cap IPO marketing could test appetite for richly-valued tech
OPEC+ monthly meeting (ongoing) — oil supply dynamics, margin implications for energy & transport
Chinese inflation, balance-of-trade data (mid-week) — global growth picture
screening.risk_warning: If AAPL WWDC reveals strong AI acceleration, capex guidance surprises to upside, or CPI prints cooler-than-expected (defusing Fed tightening fears), conviction SELL thesis breaks. Similarly, if geopolitical Iran-Israel tensions de-escalate sharply (ceasefire announced), risk-off premium collapses and Nasdaq could snap back 1-2% rapidly. Watch for VXN mean reversion and any positive surprise in hyperscaler guidance (AWS, Azure) — these could flip to BUY.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended after Friday's point-drop record and ongoing 24h decline. Avoid chasing shorts into resistance; wait for retest of 29,100–29,200 or stabilization >29,000 before re-entering long. If break below 28,200 (Friday close), next downside support is 27,500—but tactical bounces likely; do not add shorts on extension.
